parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: This recording is copyright. You will hear a man speaking on the radio about recycling in a town called Bookwall. You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now, answer the questions on your screen.

Speaker 2 (Kevin): So, Kevin, can people take anything to the recycling centres in Bookwall?

Speaker 3: I'm glad you asked me. It depends. Let's start with batteries. Now, there are many different types, from the little ones that go into our car keys and small disposable ones, right through to vehicle batteries. At the moment, not all our recycling centres can take every kind, so the best advice is to inquire ahead. Phone if in doubt and explain exactly what type of battery you have, and our staff will be able to advise. It's better to do that than bring something in but then find that we can't accept it.

Speaker 2: What else?

Speaker 3: Clothes. If there's anything you don't wear anymore or you've outgrown it, we'd love to accept it, so you can just turn up whenever you want at one of our centres. Clothing can often be redistributed to those who need it, which is great.

Speaker 2: And what about building materials?

Speaker 3: You can dispose of these if it's your own small-scale renovation project, but to do this, we ask you to reserve a slot at the recycling centre with a couple of days' notice if you can, please. But this service excludes professional builders, I'm afraid. Tradespeople will need to make their own arrangements or hire a skip.

Speaker 2: What about furniture?

Speaker 3: Most of our sites can accept furniture, but this is a bit more complicated. Some of our smaller centres can't accept larger items, such as beds, so it's worth having a look on the website in advance. It tells you exactly what can be recycled and where, so you can potentially save yourselves a wasted trip.

Speaker 2: What about electronics?

Speaker 3: Goods such as laptops, phones and TVs generally have plenty of components that can be salvaged, so you can deposit these at all of our sites. So, feel free to just turn up and drop them off. No need to book an appointment. We just ask you to make sure you put them in the right skip once you're there.
